headline: WEEK AHEAD: CATTLE TEST $250 FLOOR
subheadline: Friday's livestock selloff sets the stage for a make-or-break week in the cattle complex.
lead: Cattle closed Friday at $253.00, down from Thursday's highs but still holding above the $250 level that's become the market's line in the sand. The livestock selloff that crushed hogs 9% over two days is testing whether cattle's breakout has real conviction or just borrowed momentum from energy's rally. This week's fed cattle trade and Friday's Cattle on Feed report will decide which story the market believes.
the_takeaway: Cattle at $250 — either the floor holds or the breakout was fake.
teaser: The livestock correction puts cattle's month-long rally to the test at a critical technical level.

one_number.context: Cattle held above $250 despite the livestock complex selloff that hammered hogs. The $250 level has become the market's referendum on whether April's breakout was real conviction or just energy spillover. Break it, and the whole rally story gets rewritten.

sections.title: Cattle Floor Test
sections.body: Live cattle closed Friday at $253.00, down 0.4% but crucially above the $250 technical floor that's held for three weeks. The livestock selloff that destroyed hogs couldn't break cattle's resolve, but it exposed how thin the buying conviction really is. This week's fed cattle trade typically sets the tone for May delivery, and packers haven't shown their hand since the last Cattle on Feed print. The $250 level is binary — hold it and the April breakout stays intact, break it and cattle joins hogs in the correction column.
sections.bottom_line: Binary week: $250 holds the breakout story or kills it.

sections.title: Grains Coil Tighter
sections.body: Corn finished Friday at $4.80, up a penny and marking time in the same 15-cent range that's contained the market for two weeks. Soybeans barely moved at $12.03, flat on the session. The planting window is cooperating — no weather drama to speak of — and the funds are neither adding nor cutting length in any meaningful size. Range-bound isn't boring when it's building pressure. Corn's sitting 97% up from its 52-week low with planting season providing no catalyst either direction.
sections.bottom_line: Coiled spring waiting for weather or export surprise to pick direction.

sections.title: Week Ahead Setup
sections.body: Three data points matter this week: Monday's Crop Progress (corn planting pace), Wednesday's weekly export sales, and Friday's Cattle on Feed. The calendar is loaded but the setup is clean — cattle testing support while grains mark time. Energy's pullback from triple digits removes one tailwind from the ag complex, but crude at $101.94 isn't exactly bearish for input costs. The livestock correction either spreads to cattle or stops at the $250 line.
sections.bottom_line: Clean setup: cattle binary, grains waiting, energy backdrop still supportive.

the_more_you_know.title: The Binary Level: When Markets Draw Lines in the Sand
the_more_you_know.body: Cattle's $250 level isn't arbitrary — it's where three weeks of buying conviction gets tested. Technical levels become binary when they carry narrative weight: hold and the story continues, break and the story changes. The 97% recovery in corn from its 52-week low created a similar binary at $5.00 last fall. Markets that respect their own levels build credibility. Markets that don't lose it fast.

watch_list.desc: USDA Crop Progress — corn planting above 50% keeps seasonal pressure off
Weekly export sales — corn under 800K MT, soybeans under 400K MT maintains status quo
Fed cattle trade — $188-190 cash maintains premium structure
Cattle on Feed — placements above 100% of year-ago adds supply pressure to June

daily_quote.text: Beware of little expenses. A small leak will sink a great ship.
